response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web software engineer with strong craftsmanship practices, but the role requires deep hardware-performance expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, and deep-learning workload analysis — none of which appear in the resume. His low-latency DLang systems work and brief C/C++ experience at National Instruments show systems aptitude, but there is no evidence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, hardware-software co-design, or DNN training/inference optimization. The domain gap is too large for a principal-level IC role in this specialty.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years qualification bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Broad technical range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, with strong testing discipline
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No deep-learning workload, training, or inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture or hardware-software co-design
